question: If my car keys get stolen but there's no other damage, will you cover re‑coding or replacing the keys and locks — and do I need to report it to the police?
answer: Yes. If your vehicle keys are stolen during the policy period and there is no other damage, the insurer will pay up to $1,000 (in addition to any basic excess) to replace or re‑code your vehicle’s keys, locks and barrels. You must report the theft of the keys to the police.
citation: P.14 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Comprehensive Insurance

question: If I'm not at fault in a collision and my car can't be driven, will you arrange a hire car for me while it's being repaired — and do I need to give you the other driver's name and residential address to get that benefit?
answer: Yes. If the insurer agrees to pay your collision claim, your vehicle cannot be driven (or is unsafe or in for repairs), the insurer is satisfied you were not at fault, and you can provide the name and residential address of a person the insurer agrees is at fault, they will arrange a hire car and pay for it until your vehicle is repaired or the claim for a total loss is paid. The insurer will also pay up to $2,000 in total for any excess and security bond under the hire agreement. You must not arrange your own hire car unless you get prior written consent.
citation: P.14 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Comprehensive Insurance

question: My car is only about a year old — if it's a total loss, can you replace it with a brand new one? What conditions would I need to meet and what payments (excess, outstanding premiums or consent from my finance company) would I be responsible for?
answer: Possibly. If the insurer considers your vehicle a total loss and you meet these conditions, you can choose a new replacement vehicle of the same make, model, engine size, equipment level and paint type: (1) you bought the vehicle new (or it was a demonstrator), (2) it is within the first 24 months of its registration at the time of the incident, (3) an equivalent replacement is available in Australia, (4) any credit provider shown on your Certificate of Insurance has agreed (if applicable), and (5) the vehicle’s tare weight is under 2.5 tonnes. The insurer will replace modifications/options/accessories and pay the first 12 months’ registration and CTP (if applicable). You must pay any excess that applies, any outstanding premium (including the balance of monthly instalments), and refund any registration and CTP amounts you receive relating to your vehicle. If the vehicle is replaced, your policy ends, no premium refund is due, and the replaced vehicle becomes the insurer’s property.
citation: P.14 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Comprehensive Insurance

question: If my car gets stolen or there's an attempted theft, what will you do — will you repair it or pay me, and what's the most you'll pay?
answer: If your vehicle is lost or damaged by fire, theft or attempted theft we will cover the loss up to $10,000. We will decide whether to repair your vehicle, pay the fair and reasonable cost to repair it as we determine, or pay the market value (less any applicable deductions). Our decision will take the circumstances of the claim into account and consider any preference you have. The maximum we will pay is $10,000.
citation: P.23 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Third Party Fire & Theft Insurance

question: Someone hit my car and it was their fault but they don't have insurance — will you cover my repairs and what information do you need from me?
answer: There is limited cover of up to $5,000 for damage when another vehicle causes the collision and the other driver is uninsured for your damage. To be eligible you must: show that the collision was the other driver’s fault (we must be satisfied of this), provide the other vehicle’s registration and the other driver’s name and residential address, and the damage must exceed the basic excess on your policy. You must also pay an administration charge of $300. Coverage is limited to the stated maximum and subject to those conditions.
citation: P.23 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Third Party Fire & Theft Insurance

question: If my car is stolen and can't be driven, do you provide a hire car, how much will you pay and for how long, and can I organise my own hire car instead?
answer: If we agree to your theft or attempted theft claim and your vehicle cannot be driven or is unsafe, we will arrange a hire car and pay up to $60 per day for a maximum of 21 days (or until your vehicle is recovered and repaired, or until we pay your claim if it is a total loss), whichever is shortest. We will also pay up to $2,000 in total for any hire-car excess and security bond payable under the hire agreement because of loss or damage. We do not cover running costs (for example petrol or tolls), optional extras, loss or damage to the hire car, or liability arising from its use. You must not arrange your own hire car unless you obtain our prior written consent (contact 13 19 03); if you arrange one without consent we may decide not to pay for it.
citation: P.23 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Third Party Fire & Theft Insurance

question: If another driver hits my car and they don't have any insurance, will Third Party Property Damage cover the cost to fix my car? I read there's a $5,000 limit — is that the most you'll pay, and what information do you need from me to show the other driver was at fault?
answer: Under the Uninsured motorist part of Third Party Property Damage cover, the insurer provides limited cover for damage to your vehicle when another driver (who is uninsured) is at fault. The cover is up to $5,000 for loss or damage, and applies only if the collision was with another vehicle, the other driver was uninsured for the damage, the insurer is satisfied the other driver was at fault, the damage exceeds your basic excess, and you can give the other vehicle's registration plus the other driver’s name and residential address. The insurer will then decide whether to repair the vehicle, pay the fair and reasonable repair cost as they determine it, or pay the vehicle’s market value less any deductions; that decision will take the claim circumstances and any preference you express into account.
citation: P.27 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Third Party Property Damage Insurance

question: Do I have to use an RACV Partner Repairer to get my car fixed, or can I choose my own? If I pick my own repairer, what licences, equipment or capabilities do they need to have?
answer: You are not required to use an RACV Partner Repairer; the policy gives you access to the RACV partner network but you may choose your own repairer (including for motorcycles). Any repairer you choose must have all equipment, skills, capabilities, licences and legal authorisations required by law to complete the repairs to your vehicle.
citation: P.27 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Third Party Property Damage Insurance

question: If my car can't be driven after an incident, will the policy pay to tow it — and is towing only covered if you agree to pay the claim? How many tows will you cover and where will you tow it to?
answer: Towing is covered only if the insurer agrees to pay your claim arising from the incident and your vehicle needs towing because it cannot be driven or is unsafe. The insurer will pay the reasonable cost to tow the vehicle from where the incident happened to whichever nearby place they decide is closer — either a repairer or other place they recommend, or a safe place. They only pay for one tow; if more than one tow is needed, they will choose which tow to cover.
citation: P.27 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Third Party Property Damage Insurance

question: If I'm at fault in a crash and damage someone else's vehicle, what's the most you'll pay for liability on that one incident?
answer: Liability cover will respond to a claim for loss or damage you cause to someone else’s property, and the insurer’s maximum payment for any one incident is $20,000,000. They will also pay legal costs when their lawyers act in connection with the claim.
citation: P.29 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Cover for all types of Insurance

question: If I lend my car to a friend and they're driving when they hit someone's fence, will my policy cover them or do they need my permission to be covered?
answer: The policy will cover drivers other than you, but to be entitled to liability cover the other driver must have had your permission to drive the vehicle at the time of the incident. Passengers likewise must have had your permission (or the permission of someone entitled to let them be in the vehicle).
citation: P.29 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Cover for all types of Insurance

question: If my car is involved in an incident and I need legal representation for a coroner's inquiry, will you pay for a lawyer and is there a limit or anything I need to do first?
answer: Yes — the insurer will pay the reasonable costs of legal representation if you (or a permitted driver) need to be represented before a coroner conducting an inquiry. The maximum payable for any legal representation is $10,000. You must contact the insurer before incurring those costs, and costs related to penalties/fines or where you or the permitted driver have been charged with an offence are not covered.
citation: P.29 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Cover for all types of Insurance

question: If someone hacks my car’s alarm or GPS and then my car gets stolen, will you still cover the theft?
answer: If a cyber attack makes your GPS or security system unusable, the insurer won't cover the failure of those electronic systems or loss of use. However, if the vehicle is physically stolen after its security system has been compromised by a cyber attack, the theft itself will be covered.
citation: P.31 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Things that may  affect cover

question: I lent my car to a mate and he was caught drink‑driving — will you refuse my claim because of that?
answer: If the driver was under the influence at the time of the incident, the insurer will refuse any claim unless you can show you were not the driver and had no reason to suspect the driver was impaired. Even if damage to your vehicle is paid, the intoxicated driver will have no liability cover and the insurer may seek to recover amounts from that driver.
citation: P.31 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Things that may  affect cover

question: I’ve just moved and my car is now kept at a different address — do I need to tell you, and what happens if I don’t?
answer: Yes — you must tell the insurer if you change the address where your vehicle is normally kept. If you fail to notify them and that change causes or contributes to an incident, they may refuse or reduce a claim or cancel your policy; they'll consider the action taken based on the effect your nondisclosure had on the claim or their decision to issue cover.
citation: P.31 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Things that may  affect cover

question: If I need to make a claim, can I call anytime — is the claims line open 24 hours a day, 7 days a week and what's the number?
answer: Yes. The claims service is available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week — you can call 13 19 03 to make a claim.
citation: P.35 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Claiming under your Policy

question: When I make a claim, what kind of information will you ask for — will you ask a series of questions over the phone or ask me to provide detailed written information?
answer: When you lodge a claim they may either ask you a series of questions or request detailed written information, and they will provide immediate assistance with the claim.
citation: P.35 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Claiming under your Policy

question: If there's an excess to pay, will you tell me that when I lodge the claim and explain how I can pay it?
answer: Yes. They will tell you if an excess applies and explain how you can pay it.
citation: P.35 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Claiming under your Policy

question: Do I have to report a theft or vandalism to the police and keep the incident number before you’ll accept my claim?
answer: Yes. You are required to report any theft, attempted theft, vandalism or malicious act to the police and keep the incident report number they give you. Failing to meet this and your other claim responsibilities can lead us to refuse or reduce your claim payment, or cancel your policy depending on the effect of the failure.
citation: P.36 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Your responsibilities when making a claim under this Policy

question: If you ask to inspect my car or see receipts for accessories, do I have to let you do that and send those documents as soon as possible?
answer: Yes. If requested, you must allow us to inspect your vehicle at a reasonable time and provide proof of ownership or details for items, modifications or accessories (for example receipts, valuations, model or serial numbers) as soon as reasonably possible. You must cooperate fully and supply any information or documents we need to handle your claim.
citation: P.36 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Your responsibilities when making a claim under this Policy

question: Am I allowed to settle with the other driver or admit fault myself, or do I need your permission first?
answer: You must not attempt to settle a claim made against you without our permission, nor make any admissions to anyone about an incident covered by your policy.
citation: P.36 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Your responsibilities when making a claim under this Policy

question: What's an excess and how is it applied when I make a claim — do you deduct it from the payout or ask me to pay the repairer?
answer: An excess is the portion you contribute towards the cost of a claim (there are basic, age and special excesses). When a claim is made, the excess may either be deducted from any claim payment or you may be asked to pay the excess to us or directly to the repairer/supplier; if payment is requested we’ll tell you who to pay and may require it to finalise the claim.
citation: P.37 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Excesses

question: If someone else hits my car and it's clearly not my fault, what do I need to give you to avoid having to pay the excess?
answer: You won’t have to pay an excess if we are satisfied the incident was not your (or your driver’s or passenger’s) fault and you can provide the name and residential address of the person(s) we agree is at fault, and the claim amount is more than any basic excess.
citation: P.37 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Excesses

question: I turned 25 last year but only got my full licence 18 months ago — would I still have to pay an age excess if I make a claim?
answer: Yes. An age excess applies if the driver was under 25, or is 25 or older but has less than two years’ driving experience after obtaining a provisional or probationary licence. With only 18 months’ experience after getting your licence, an age excess would apply.
citation: P.37 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Excesses

question: If you agree to repair my car, can I choose my own repairer or will you arrange one for me?
answer: If we agree to repair your vehicle, we will normally arrange for an RACV Partner Repairer to do the work. You may be able to choose your own repairer in specific situations: if you have Comprehensive cover and selected the ‘Any Repairer’ option, if the vehicle is a motorcycle, or if you have Third Party Fire & Theft or Third Party Property Damage cover. When you choose your own repairer you must ensure they have the required licences, equipment and provide a quote and allow us to inspect the vehicle before authorising repairs.
citation: P.39 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - How we settle a claim

question: What happens if I start or approve repairs before getting your written consent — will you still pay for them?
answer: You must not start, approve or authorise repairs unless we permit it under emergency repairs or you first obtain our prior written consent. If you go ahead without our written consent, we may decide not to pay for those repairs. Also, if we have already cash-settled your claim (paid the fair and reasonable cost to repair) and you then arrange or approve repairs, we are not responsible for those repair costs.
citation: P.39 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - How we settle a claim

question: If my car is declared a total loss on my Comprehensive policy, what will you pay me and do you keep the vehicle?
answer: If your vehicle is a total loss under Comprehensive cover, we will pay either the agreed value or the market value as shown on your Certificate of Insurance, after deducting any unpaid premium, any applicable excesses and the unused portions of registration and compulsory third party insurance you are entitled to. If we pay a total loss, your policy ends (no premium refund) and the vehicle becomes our property.
citation: P.39 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - How we settle a claim

question: I still owe money on my car — if it’s written off, will you pay the finance company instead of me, and how do you work out how much they get?
answer: If the finance company (credit provider) is recorded as having an interest in the vehicle, we can pay the credit provider instead of you. The amount paid will be the lesser of: the agreed or market value under your cover (less any deductions); the fair and reasonable cost of repairs as we determine (less any deductions); or the outstanding balance owing to the credit provider. If the credit provider is entitled to the vehicle’s salvage, we will deduct the estimated salvage value from any payment.
citation: P.45 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Credit provider’s rights

question: Do I have to list my leasing or hire‑purchase company on the Certificate of Insurance so they’re covered too?
answer: You may be required to note the credit provider’s interest if you lease or have a hire‑purchase agreement. If the credit provider is listed on your current Certificate of Insurance, the cover you have chosen extends to them, subject to the same conditions and exclusions.
citation: P.45 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Credit provider’s rights

question: What happens if my lender doesn’t provide the information you ask for — could that affect whether my claim gets paid?
answer: Yes. The insurer’s ability to pay the claim may be affected if the credit provider fails to provide the information requested.
citation: P.45 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Credit provider’s rights

question: How do you work out my car insurance premium — what things do you take into account when setting my price?
answer: Your premium is set according to the likelihood of a future claim on your policy. Factors considered include the vehicle itself, where it is kept, the ages of owners and drivers, and the number and types of prior claims or incidents. Discounts (for example Multi‑Policy, Years of Membership and Flexi Excess) are calculated before your premium is quoted and any discounts applied will be shown on your Certificate of Insurance. For full details you can refer to the Motor Insurance Premium, Excess and Discounts Guide or contact RACV.
citation: P.48 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - How we work out your premium

question: If I have two RACV policies, do I automatically get the Multi-Policy Discount and will that be shown on my Certificate of Insurance?
answer: The Multi‑Policy Discount applies to your Motor Insurance if you hold two or more eligible RACV Insurance policies. Discounts that apply are calculated before your premium is quoted, and any discounts applied will be listed on your current Certificate of Insurance.
citation: P.48 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - How we work out your premium

question: How does the colour of my RACV membership card affect the Years of Membership Benefit, and who should I contact if I have questions about my card colour?
answer: The Years of Membership Benefit is linked to the colour of your RACV membership card. If you have questions about the colour of your membership card you should contact RACV (for example, RACV Insurance on 13 RACV / 13 7228) for assistance.
citation: P.48 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - How we work out your premium

question: Can I pay my premium by monthly instalments using a credit card or does it have to be from a bank account?
answer: You can pay by monthly instalments by direct debit from either a nominated bank account or a nominated credit card, subject to eligibility.
citation: P.49 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Paying your premium - what you should know

question: If one of my monthly instalments can't be deducted, will you send me a notice before cancelling my policy?
answer: Yes. If an instalment cannot be deducted you will be sent a notice detailing the overdue amount and when it must be paid. If the instalment remains unpaid, a second notice will be sent either before cancellation advising the effective cancellation date or within 14 days after cancellation confirming that date.
citation: P.49 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Paying your premium - what you should know

question: I'm making a claim but my policy's overdue — do I have to pay the overdue amount before you settle the claim?
answer: If your policy is overdue but not yet cancelled, you will be required to pay the overdue amount as part of the claim settlement. Alternatively, where the settlement method allows, the insurer can reduce the settlement payment by the overdue amount.
citation: P.49 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Paying your premium - what you should know

question: If I sell my car, does my insurance end straight away or do I need to tell you first – and will I get any refund?
answer: When you sell or give away your vehicle the Policy ends immediately and you will no longer be covered; the policy comes to an end without any notice to you. If you ask for a refund, the insurer will deduct an amount that covers the period you have already been insured for and pay you the remainder. The policy also notes that, in general, if you want to make changes to your Policy you need to contact the insurer first and pay any additional premium required.
citation: P.50 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Making changes to  your Policy

question: I just bought a replacement vehicle — if I swap it within 14 days, is it automatically covered and for how long? And what happens to the agreed value if I have agreed value cover?
answer: If you replace your vehicle with a similar one within 14 days of selling or giving the old vehicle away, the insurer provides temporary cover for the replacement vehicle on the same Policy terms for 14 days from the date of purchase. If you have agreed value cover, the agreed value for the replacement vehicle will be its purchase price. To have the replacement vehicle covered permanently under the Policy you must, within the 14‑day temporary period, ask the insurer to cover it, provide full details, obtain the insurer's agreement, and pay any additional premium required.
citation: P.50 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Making changes to  your Policy

question: If I want to make a change to my policy, do I have to pay any extra premium straight away? And is it true you waive extra premiums under $15?
answer: If you request a change that attracts an additional premium you are required to pay that additional premium when the change is made. If the insurer makes the change before you have paid, you must pay by the date they tell you or they may remove the change or, if they cannot, cancel the Policy. If the additional premium required is less than $15, the insurer will waive it and you do not need to pay.
citation: P.50 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Making changes to  your Policy

question: If I’m unhappy with how my claim was handled, should I call 13 RACV or pop into my local RACV Shop first?
answer: Start by contacting RACV either by calling 13 RACV (13 7228) or by visiting your local RACV Shop and speaking with a staff member.
citation: P.52 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - How to resolve a  complaint or dispute

question: What happens if the staff member can’t resolve my complaint — will it be referred to a manager and then to the Member Relations Department for a review?
answer: Yes. If the staff member cannot resolve the matter they will refer it to a manager. If it remains unresolved the manager will refer your complaint to the RACV Member Relations Department, who will arrange a review.
citation: P.52 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - How to resolve a  complaint or dispute

question: If I’m still not satisfied after the Member Relations review, how do I get an external review and what’s the AFCA phone number?
answer: You are entitled to seek an external review; you will be given information about the available options and may be referred to the external dispute resolution scheme run by the Australian Financial Complaints Authority. AFCA can be contacted on 1800 931 678 (free call).
citation: P.52 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - How to resolve a  complaint or dispute

question: What does the General Insurance Code of Practice actually mean for me as a policyholder?
answer: The Code is intended to lift industry standards and commits the insurer to high levels of service. For you as a policyholder it means the insurer aims to promote better, more informed relations with you, maintain trust and confidence in general insurance, provide fair and effective ways to resolve complaints, and encourage ongoing improvement through education and training. The insurer has adopted the Code and is committed to complying with it.
citation: P.52 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - General Insurance Code  of Practice

question: If I have a complaint about a claim or your service, how does the Code make sure there are fair and effective ways to resolve it?
answer: The Code requires insurers to provide fair and effective mechanisms for resolving complaints you make. Oversight is provided by an independent Code Governance Committee that monitors and enforces insurers’ compliance with the Code, helping ensure those complaint processes are upheld. The insurer also states it has adopted the Code and is committed to complying with it.
citation: P.52 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - General Insurance Code  of Practice

question: Who is the Code Governance Committee and can I contact you or them if I want more information about how you comply with the Code?
answer: The Code Governance Committee is an independent body that monitors and enforces insurers’ compliance with the Code. You are invited to contact the insurer if you would like more information about the Code or about the Code Governance Committee.
citation: P.52 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - General Insurance Code  of Practice

question: If my insurer goes insolvent, am I entitled to payment under the Financial Claims Scheme?
answer: You may be entitled to a payment if the insurer becomes insolvent, but receiving a payment depends on meeting the scheme’s eligibility criteria.
citation: P.52 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Financial Claims Scheme

question: How can I check whether I’m eligible for a payment under the Financial Claims Scheme?
answer: Eligibility is determined by the scheme’s criteria; you should consult the scheme’s information to see if you meet those requirements.
citation: P.52 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Financial Claims Scheme

question: Where can I find more information about the Financial Claims Scheme and how to access it?
answer: More information, including how to access the scheme, is available from the scheme’s website at www.fcs.gov.au.
citation: P.52 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Financial Claims Scheme

question: If I don’t want to give you some of my personal information, will that stop me getting cover or affect my ability to make a claim?
answer: You can choose not to provide some personal information, but doing so may affect the insurer’s ability to provide a product or service to you — which can include providing cover or handling your claim.
citation: P.53 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Privacy of your personal information

question: Who do you share my personal information with — could it be disclosed to related companies or service providers overseas?
answer: Your personal information may be disclosed to related entities, agents and distributors, and to service providers — including some service providers that may be based overseas — as well as other parties described in the Policies.
citation: P.53 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Privacy of your personal information

question: How can I get a copy of the RACV Privacy Charter or your Privacy Policy, and how do I request access or correction of my personal information if I need to?
answer: You can get a free copy of the RACV Privacy Charter by visiting racv.com.au, calling 13 RACV (13 7228) or visiting an RACV shop. The insurer’s Privacy Policy is available at iag.com.au/master-privacy-policy. The Policies also explain how you can access or ask for correction of your personal information and how to make a privacy complaint.
citation: P.53 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Privacy of your personal information

question: If my car's declared a total loss and I've got an agreed value on my certificate, will you deduct the excess and any registration/CTP refund from that agreed value?
answer: Yes. When a vehicle is a total loss and an agreed value is shown on the Certificate of Insurance, the insurer pays that agreed value less the policy excess and less any registration and compulsory third party (CTP) refund — for example, $14,200 agreed value minus $700 excess and $250 registration/CTP refund = $13,250 paid to you. Towing is treated separately and is paid to the towing company.
citation: P.55 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Definitions

question: If my car's towed after an accident and sent to a repairer, do I have to pay the excess straight to the repairer and will you pay the towing bill?
answer: Yes. If your vehicle is towed to a repairer, you pay the applicable excess to the repairer (for example, you pay a $700 excess and the insurer pays the repairer the repair balance). The insurer will pay the towing company’s invoice separately (for example, $350 paid to the towing company).
citation: P.55 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Definitions

question: If I fit aftermarket accessories or make modifications to my car, are those included in the agreed value or market value you use to settle a claim?
answer: Yes. Both the agreed value and the market value include any modifications, options or accessories that are attached to the vehicle (along with GST, registration, CTP where applicable and other on‑road costs).
citation: P.55 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Definitions

question: If my car is written off by a fire under Third Party Fire & Theft, how do you work out what you pay me — do you subtract the damaged vehicle value, my $700 excess and any unpaid premium? Can I keep the damaged vehicle?
answer: Yes. In that example the insurer deducts the damaged vehicle value, the $700 excess and any unpaid premium from the vehicle’s market value (for example $12,000 − $3,000 − $700 − $50 = $8,250). You keep the damaged vehicle.
citation: P.57 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Claim payment examples

question: If another driver who isn’t insured totally wrecks my car, how will my payout be calculated — is it just the market value minus the damaged vehicle value, and will I still keep the car?
answer: Yes. For an uninsured-at-fault total loss they pay the market value minus the damaged vehicle value (for example $4,200 − $100 = $4,100). You keep the damaged vehicle.
citation: P.57 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Claim payment examples

question: If I’m found liable for damage to someone else’s property and you pay $5,000 to them and $1,500 to the lawyers, am I only responsible for the $700 excess or are there any other costs I’d have to cover?
answer: You are required to pay the $700 excess. The insurer pays the $5,000 to the claimant and $1,500 to lawyers in that example.
citation: P.57 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Claim payment examples

question: If someone steals my car or tries to, am I entitled to a hire car while mine is being sorted out and how do I make a claim for that?
answer: If your vehicle is stolen or there is an attempted theft, the Index shows that entitlement to a hire car is addressed for comprehensive cover (and is also referenced under third party fire & theft). To arrange this and lodge a claim you would follow the policy’s “how to make a claim” procedure outlined in the claims section.
citation: P.60 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Index

question: Can you explain how the excess works — what's the basic excess and when would an age excess or special excess apply?
answer: The Index indicates the policy distinguishes a basic excess and additional excess types called an age excess and a special excess. The policy sets out what the basic excess is and explains in which circumstances the age excess or a special excess will apply, as well as when excesses do not apply.
citation: P.60 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Index

question: If my car is damaged, can I choose any repairer or do I have to use an RACV partner repairer, and what happens if the vehicle cannot be repaired?
answer: The Index shows there are options: the policy refers to using any RACV partner repairer for comprehensive (and the partner repairer reference also appears for third party covers) and also refers to an “any repairer” option and sections on choosing a repairer and repairing your vehicle. If the vehicle cannot be repaired, the policy contains a specific section explaining what happens in that situation.
citation: P.60 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Index

question: If my car gets stolen, will the policy provide a hire car while mine is being repaired or replaced?
answer: Yes. The policy summary lists “Hire car after a theft” as a feature, so a hire car is provided after your vehicle is stolen (subject to the type of cover you choose).
citation: P.11 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Features & Benefits

question: If I take the windscreen option, do I have to pay an excess for windscreen or window glass repairs/replacement?
answer: No. The summary shows a “Windscreen option (with no excess)”, and it also lists cover for windscreen and window glass, so choosing the windscreen option means there is no excess for windscreen/window glass repair or replacement.
citation: P.11 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Features & Benefits

question: Are modifications I've made to my car automatically covered under the policy or do I need to notify you before an incident?
answer: Modifications are shown as automatically covered—the summary includes “Automatic cover for modifications” (availability may depend on the type of cover you choose).
citation: P.11 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Features & Benefits

question: If I cancel the policy within 21 days and I haven't made any claims, will you refund my premium in full?
answer: Yes. If you tell us within 21 days of the policy being issued and you have not made any claims, we will refund the full premium you have paid.
citation: P.12 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Our agreement with you

question: Can I appoint my partner or a family member to manage a claim for me — do you need to agree first and will you ever unreasonably withhold consent?
answer: You can appoint someone to manage your claim, but you must tell us and we need to agree to deal with them on your behalf. Appointing a family member is usually acceptable and we will not unreasonably withhold consent. We may decline if there is a potential conflict of interest (for example, someone who supplies goods or services for your claim). Also, you must not assign policy benefits, rights or obligations without our written consent.
citation: P.12 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Our agreement with you

question: If you email my policy documents, when are they considered received, and what should I do if my email or postal address changes?
answer: If we email your policy documents to the last email address you gave us, they are considered received 24 hours after we send them. If we post them, we send to the last mailing address you gave us. You are responsible for keeping both your email and mailing address up to date and should tell us of any change as soon as possible.
citation: P.12 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Our agreement with you

question: If I hold Comprehensive Insurance, am I covered for damage I cause to someone else's property?
answer: Yes. Comprehensive Insurance includes liability cover for damage you cause to someone else’s property (see page 22).
citation: P.14 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Comprehensive Insurance

question: Does Comprehensive Insurance include legal representation if I need it?
answer: Yes. Comprehensive Insurance includes legal representation (see page 23).
citation: P.14 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Comprehensive Insurance

question: Where can I find the full details of what Comprehensive Insurance covers — you mentioned pages 7 to 15; which pages cover the liability and legal representation benefits?
answer: The liability benefit is detailed on page 22 and the legal representation benefit is on page 23.
citation: P.14 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Comprehensive Insurance

question: If my car gets damaged in a flood or a storm, is that covered under my policy?
answer: Yes. Flood and storm are specifically listed as incidents that the policy will cover for loss or damage to your vehicle.
citation: P.14 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Cover for loss or damage

question: If my car is in an accident, do you decide whether to repair it, pay the fair and reasonable cost of repairing it, or pay the agreed or market value?
answer: Yes. The insurer will determine whether to repair the vehicle, pay the fair and reasonable cost of repairs as they assess it, or pay the agreed or market value, depending on the cover shown on your current Certificate of Insurance; their choice will consider the circumstances of the claim and any preference you express.
citation: P.14 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Cover for loss or damage

question: Does the policy cover theft or attempted theft, and how do you decide whether to repair the car or pay the agreed/market value listed on my Certificate of Insurance?
answer: Yes. Theft and attempted theft are covered. The insurer will decide to either repair the vehicle, pay the fair and reasonable cost of repairs as they determine, or pay the agreed or market value (as set out on your Certificate of Insurance), taking into account the claim circumstances and any preference you have; payments are subject to applicable deductions.
citation: P.14 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Cover for loss or damage

question: If only my windscreen is cracked, will you repair or replace it or just pay me the cost — and do I have to pay the basic excess?
answer: If only the windscreen (or sunroof or window glass) is damaged, the insurer will either repair or replace it or pay the cost of repair or replacement. You must pay any basic excess for that glass-only claim, but you will not be charged an age or special excess.
citation: P.14 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Windscreen, sunroof & window glass damage

question: I noticed a Windscreen option mentioned on page 15 — does that let me get windscreen or window glass repairs/replacement without paying the basic excess?
answer: Yes. Under Comprehensive cover, the Windscreen option (referenced on page 15) provides cover for windscreen, sunroof and window glass repairs or replacement without needing to pay the basic excess.
citation: P.14 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Windscreen, sunroof & window glass damage

question: Will I ever have to pay an age or special excess for glass-only damage, or are there other exclusions or limits I should check (like the things on pages 24–27)?
answer: You will not have to pay an age or special excess for glass-only damage. However, you should check the "Things that may affect cover" on pages 24–27 for important exclusions or limitations that could apply to the cover described on pages 7–15.
citation: P.14 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Windscreen, sunroof & window glass damage

question: If my car keys are stolen but there's no other damage to the vehicle, will you cover replacing or re-coding the keys, locks and barrels?
answer: Yes. If your vehicle’s keys are stolen and there is no other damage to the vehicle, the insurer will cover replacing or re-coding the vehicle’s keys, locks and barrels.
citation: P.15 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Keys, locks & barrels

question: How much will you pay for replacing/re-coding the keys and is that amount over and above my basic excess or do I still have to pay the excess?
answer: The insurer will pay up to $1,000 for replacing or re-coding the keys, locks and barrels, and that payment is provided over and above any basic excess that applies to your policy.
citation: P.15 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Keys, locks & barrels

question: Do I have to report the theft of my keys to the police in order to make a claim for this cover?
answer: Yes. You are required to report the theft of the keys to the police.
citation: P.15 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Keys, locks & barrels

question: My policy mentions access to RACV’s partner repairer network — do I have to use one of their repairers or can I pick my own if I’ve got the ‘Any Repairer’ option?
answer: Your policy gives you automatic access to RACV’s partner repairer network, but you may choose your own repairer if you have selected the ‘Any Repairer’ option.
citation: P.15 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Any RACV Partner Repairer

question: I ride a motorcycle — does that automatically let me choose my own repairer even if I didn’t select the ‘Any Repairer’ option?
answer: Yes. If your vehicle is a motorcycle, you may choose your own repairer even if you have not chosen the ‘Any Repairer’ option.
citation: P.15 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Any RACV Partner Repairer

question: If I choose my own repairer, what equipment, skills, licences or authorisations do they need to have to be acceptable under the policy?
answer: Your chosen repairer must have all necessary equipment, skills, capabilities, licences and legal authorisations required by law to complete the repairs to your vehicle.
citation: P.15 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Any RACV Partner Repairer

question: If I make a claim for something listed under 'Cover for loss or damage' on page 7, am I entitled to any extra cover?
answer: The document states that if you make a claim for an incident described on page 7 under ‘Cover for loss or damage’, you may be entitled to one or more additional covers.
citation: P.16 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - – Additional cover

question: Are those additional covers automatically applied when I lodge a claim for an incident on page 7, or do I have to meet other criteria to get them?
answer: The entry says you “may be entitled” to additional covers but does not state that they are automatically applied or list any eligibility criteria, so the document does not specify whether other conditions must be met.
citation: P.16 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - – Additional cover

question: Could I be eligible for more than one of the additional covers if my claim matches an incident described on page 7?
answer: Yes. The text indicates you may be entitled to one or more of the additional covers, so you could be eligible for more than one.
citation: P.16 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - – Additional cover

question: If I'm clearly not at fault and my car can't be driven while it's being repaired, will you arrange a hire car for me and how long will you pay for it?
answer: If we accept your collision claim, your vehicle (except motorcycles) can’t be driven, is unsafe or is in for repairs, you can name the person we agree is at fault, and you need a hire car, we will arrange one and try to meet your needs. We will pay for the hire car until whichever happens first: your vehicle is repaired, or we pay your claim as a total loss.
citation: P.16 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Hire car after a not at fault collision

question: Do you cover running costs or extras for the hire car—like petrol, tolls or adding a GPS?
answer: No. Running costs (for example petrol or tolls) and additional hire car costs (for example optional extras such as a GPS) are not covered.
citation: P.16 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Hire car after a not at fault collision

question: Can I organise my own hire car after the accident and get reimbursed, or do I need to get your written consent first?
answer: You must not arrange your own hire car unless you obtain our prior written consent. If you arrange a hire car without that written consent, we may decide not to pay for it.
citation: P.16 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Hire car after a not at fault collision

question: If my car gets stolen and it's not safe to drive, will you provide a hire car — and how much will you pay per day and for how long?
answer: If we accept your claim for theft or attempted theft and your vehicle cannot be driven or is unsafe, we will arrange a hire car for you and pay up to $60 per day. Cover is for up to 21 days, or until your vehicle is recovered and repaired, or until we pay your claim if the vehicle is a total loss, whichever is shortest.
citation: P.17 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Hire car after theft or attempted theft

question: Do you cover running costs or optional extras for the hire car, like petrol, tolls or a GPS?
answer: No. Running costs (for example petrol or tolls) and additional hire car costs (for example optional extras such as a GPS) are not covered.
citation: P.17 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Hire car after theft or attempted theft

question: Can I arrange a hire car myself and claim it back later, or do I need your prior written consent? And will you cover the hire car excess or security bond if the hire car is damaged?
answer: You must not arrange your own hire car unless you obtain our prior written consent by contacting 13 19 03; if you arrange one without consent we may decide not to pay for it. We will pay up to $2,000 in total for any excess and security bond payable under the hire car agreement because of loss or damage to the hire car or caused by it. Note that loss or damage to the hire car itself and liability from its use are not otherwise covered.
citation: P.17 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Hire car after theft or attempted theft

question: If my car is written off and I bought it new 20 months ago, can I get a brand-new replacement of the same make, model and specs?
answer: Yes. If your vehicle is a total loss and you bought it new (or it was a demonstrator) and it is within the first 24 months of registration (20 months qualifies), you can choose a replacement provided a matching vehicle (same make, model, engine size, equipment level and paint type) is available in Australia, any credit provider noted on your Certificate of Insurance has agreed if required, and the vehicle’s tare weight is under 2.5 tonnes.
citation: P.17 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - New replacement vehicle

question: I fitted aftermarket upgrades and accessories — will those be replaced on the new vehicle too?
answer: Yes. The insurer will replace any modifications, options or accessories on the replacement vehicle.
citation: P.17 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - New replacement vehicle

question: If you replace my vehicle, what do I need to pay and what happens to my policy and the old car?
answer: You must pay any excess that applies, any outstanding premium including the balance of monthly instalments, and any refund you receive from the registration and Compulsory Third Party Insurance on your old vehicle. If the vehicle is replaced, your policy ends with no refund of premium due, and the insured (old) vehicle becomes the insurer’s property. The insurer will also pay the first 12 months’ registration and CTP on the replacement vehicle, if applicable.
citation: P.17 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - New replacement vehicle

question: If my car’s taken to a storage yard after an accident, will you pay the storage fees from when I lodge the claim until you assess it?
answer: Yes. If we agree to pay your claim arising from the incident and your vehicle is in a storage facility or holding yard, we will pay the reasonable storage charges from the time you lodge the claim until we assess the vehicle.
citation: P.18 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Storage costs

question: Do I need to get your approval before arranging storage — will you cover fees if I arrange storage without asking first?
answer: You must have our agreement to pay the storage costs before you make any storage arrangements; without that prior agreement we will not cover those fees.
citation: P.18 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Storage costs

question: If you ask me to move my vehicle from the storage yard, do I have to do it, and can you move it yourself if needed?
answer: Yes. You are required to move the vehicle if we ask you to, and we also have the right to move your vehicle ourselves if necessary.
citation: P.18 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Storage costs

question: If someone breaks into my car and steals my phone, will the policy cover replacing it?
answer: No. Mobile phones are specifically listed as not covered under this policy. While the policy does cover personal items stolen as part of a theft or attempted theft of your vehicle in general, mobile phones are excluded from cover.
citation: P.18 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Personal items

question: If my clothes and a bag in the car are damaged in an accident, how much will you pay to repair or replace them?
answer: For vehicles other than motorcycles, the policy will pay up to $500 in total to repair or replace personal items damaged in an incident.
citation: P.18 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Personal items

question: I ride a motorcycle — if my helmet and jacket are destroyed in a crash, what's the maximum you'll pay for protective clothing and is there a per-item limit?
answer: For motorcycles, the policy will pay up to $2,000 in total to repair or replace protective clothing designed for riding. The most payable for any one item of protective clothing is $1,000 (protective clothing includes helmet and jacket).
citation: P.18 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Personal items

question: If you agree to pay my claim after an incident and my car can’t be driven or is unsafe, will you cover the towing cost to a repairer or a safe place?
answer: Yes. If the claim is accepted and the vehicle cannot be driven or is unsafe, the insurer will pay the reasonable cost to tow the vehicle from the incident location to the repairer or other place they recommend, or to a safe place.
citation: P.19 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Towing

question: If my car is written off, is the towing cost paid on top of the agreed or market value payout?
answer: Yes. The towing cost is paid as additional cover on top of your agreed or market value payout.
citation: P.19 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Towing

question: What happens if my car needs to be towed more than once after the incident — will you cover multiple tows or just one?
answer: They only pay for one tow. If more than one tow is needed, the insurer will choose which single tow to pay for.
citation: P.19 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Towing

question: If someone breaks into my car and steals my handbag and phone, are those personal items covered under the policy?
answer: Yes. The policy covers personal items stolen as part of a theft or attempted theft of your vehicle. If the items are stolen as part of some other covered incident, they must be stolen at the scene of that incident when it happens for cover to apply.
citation: P.19 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Baby capsules & child seats

question: If my baby capsule or child seat is damaged in an accident, will you replace it and how much would you pay per item?
answer: If we agree to pay your claim and a baby capsule or child seat in your vehicle is stolen or damaged because of the incident, we will contribute up to $500 per item toward replacing each baby capsule or child seat.
citation: P.19 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Baby capsules & child seats

question: If my car needs towing after an incident, will you pay for the tow and what happens if it needs more than one tow?
answer: If we agree to pay your claim and your vehicle cannot be driven or is unsafe, we will pay the reasonable cost to tow it to a repairer we choose or recommend, or to a safe place; this is paid on top of your agreed or market value. We only pay for one tow; if the vehicle needs towing more than once, we will decide which single tow to pay for.
citation: P.19 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Baby capsules & child seats

question: If my caravan gets damaged while it's hitched to my car, will you pay to repair or replace it?
answer: Yes. If the insurer agrees to your claim and your trailer or caravan is damaged while attached to or being towed by your vehicle, they will contribute toward repairing or replacing it (subject to the stated limit).
citation: P.20 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Trailer or caravan damage

question: Is the $1,000 the maximum you'll contribute towards repairing or replacing my trailer or caravan?
answer: Yes. The policy states they will pay up to $1,000 toward repairing or replacing your trailer or caravan, which is the maximum contribution under that wording.
citation: P.20 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Trailer or caravan damage

question: Are the contents inside my trailer or caravan covered if they get damaged while I'm towing it?
answer: No. Damage to the contents of the trailer or caravan is not covered.
citation: P.20 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Trailer or caravan damage

question: If I have to get emergency repairs after an incident, how much will you reimburse and do I have to pay for them up front?
answer: If the insurer agrees to pay your claim and you make emergency repairs so you can get back on the road safely, they will reimburse you up to $800 in total for those emergency repairs. You must pay for the emergency repairs up front and provide the receipts unless the insurer agrees otherwise, and the repairs must be directly related to the incident.
citation: P.20 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Emergency repairs

question: How do you decide what counts as an 'emergency repair' — for example, would fixing a broken window or a flat tyre to keep the car secure/get me back on the road be covered?
answer: The insurer will determine what counts as an emergency repair. Emergency repairs must be made to keep your vehicle secure or to get you back on the road safely, and must be directly related to the incident you are claiming for. Repairs such as a broken window or a flat tyre would only be treated as emergency repairs if they meet those conditions and the insurer determines they qualify.
citation: P.20 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Emergency repairs

question: What if the emergency repairs end up costing more than $800 — will you cover the extra or do I need to get your agreement first?
answer: Reimbursement for emergency repairs is limited to up to $800 in total. The provided entry does not state whether amounts above $800 will be covered or whether you must obtain the insurer's agreement before incurring costs above that limit.
citation: P.20 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Emergency repairs

question: If my car's badly damaged in an accident more than 100 km from home and can't be driven, will you cover getting me, my passengers and our pets home and paying for a hotel room? How much will you pay and do I need to get your approval before arranging anything?
answer: Yes. If your vehicle is covered for the incident and is damaged so it cannot be driven more than 100 km from home, the insurer will pay additional reasonable costs (on top of your vehicle’s agreed or market value) for getting you, your passengers and your domestic pets home or to another agreed destination, for temporary accommodation (room rate only) for you, your passengers and pets, and for transporting your vehicle from the repair location to your home or from the incident site to a repairer near your home. The total payment for these combined costs is limited to $1,000. You must get the insurer’s agreement to those costs before they are incurred.
citation: P.20 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Emergency transport and accommodation

question: If my car is stolen, will you provide a hire car and for how long? And if I'm in a collision where I'm not at fault, can I also get a hire car — what details do you need about the other driver?
answer: If you have the Hire Car option, the insurer will pay hire car costs for up to 21 days after a theft or attempted theft. For a collision with another vehicle, they will pay hire car costs if they are satisfied you (or your driver) is not at fault and you can provide the name and residential address of a person the insurer agrees is at fault, and you need a hire car.
citation: P.20 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Emergency transport and accommodation

question: If I add the hire car option for other incidents, what exactly do you pay for? Do you cover running costs like petrol or optional extras (GPS), and can I arrange my own hire car and claim the cost back later?
answer: With the Hire Car option for other covered incidents, the insurer will arrange a hire car and pay up to $60 per day for: either up to 14 days, or until your vehicle is repaired, or until they pay your claim if your vehicle is a total loss — whichever is shortest. They will also pay up to $2,000 in total toward any excess and security bond under the hire agreement if the hire car is lost or damaged or causes loss or damage. They do not cover running costs (for example petrol or tolls), additional hire costs such as optional extras (for example GPS), loss or damage to the hire car, or liability from using the hire car. You must not arrange your own hire car unless you obtain the insurer’s prior written consent; if you arrange one without that consent, they may refuse to pay.
citation: P.20 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Emergency transport and accommodation

question: If you agree to pay my claim, will you automatically book an RACV Partner Repairer or can I choose any licensed repairer instead?
answer: If the claim is accepted, RACV will arrange an RACV Partner Repairer by default. However, if you have selected the 'Any Repairer' option, you may instead choose any licensed repairer you prefer, including an RACV Partner.
citation: P.22 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Any Repairer option

question: I picked the 'Any Repairer' option — does that mean I can use my local mechanic even if they're not an RACV Partner, and what qualifications do they need?
answer: Yes. Choosing 'Any Repairer' lets you use any licensed repairer, including a local mechanic who is not an RACV Partner, provided that your chosen repairer has the necessary equipment, skills, capabilities, licences and authorisations required by law to complete the repairs.
citation: P.22 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Any Repairer option

question: If I use a non‑partner repairer, will you still pay for the repairs as long as they have the necessary equipment, skills, licences and authorisations required by law?
answer: If your claim is agreed and you have chosen the 'Any Repairer' option, you may use a non‑partner repairer; your chosen repairer must have the necessary equipment, skills, capabilities, licences and authorisations required by law to complete the repairs.
citation: P.22 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Any Repairer option

question: If my car gets stolen, how many days of a hire car will you cover under the comprehensive policy?
answer: Under comprehensive cover we pay for a hire car for up to 21 days following a theft or attempted theft.
citation: P.21 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Hire Car option

question: If I'm in an accident and it's not my fault, do you still need me to give you the other driver's name and residential address before you'll pay for a hire car?
answer: Yes. We will pay hire car costs after a collision only if we are satisfied you (or your driver) are not at fault and you can provide the name and residential address of a person we agree is at fault, and you need a hire car.
citation: P.21 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Hire Car option

question: Can I just arrange a hire car myself while my car's being repaired and expect you to reimburse the hire cost and things like petrol or a GPS, or do I need to get your permission first?
answer: You must obtain our prior written consent before arranging your own hire car; if you do not, we may decide not to pay. We will arrange a hire car for you for covered incidents and pay up to $60 per day (subject to limits), but we do not cover running costs (for example petrol or tolls) or additional extras (for example a GPS).
citation: P.21 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Hire Car option

question: If I add the Hire Car option and my car gets stolen, will you cover a rental while my claim is processed—and how long would you pay for it?
answer: Yes. With the Hire Car option added, the insurer will pay for a hire car for up to 21 days following a theft or attempted theft.
citation: P.21 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - – Options

question: Can I organise my own hire car after an accident and get reimbursed, or do I have to wait for you to arrange one?
answer: You should not arrange your own hire car unless you first obtain the insurer’s prior written consent. The policy says the insurer will arrange a hire car for you; if you organise one without prior written consent they may decide not to pay for it.
citation: P.21 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - – Options

question: If I pick the Windscreen option, do I still have to pay an excess when only my windscreen or window glass is damaged?
answer: No. If you have the Windscreen option, you will not need to pay any excess when only the vehicle’s windscreen, sunroof or window glass is damaged; the insurer will repair or replace it or pay the cost of doing so.
citation: P.21 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - – Options

question: It says Third Party Fire & Theft gives the cover described on pages 16–19—can you run me through what that actually covers for my car?
answer: Unknown from document
citation: P.23 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Third Party Fire & Theft Insurance

question: The policy notes liability cover for damage I cause to someone else’s property (see page 22)—so if I clip someone’s fence or damage their garage door, would that be covered?
answer: Unknown from document
citation: P.23 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Third Party Fire & Theft Insurance

question: What does the ‘legal representation’ part mean (it points to page 23)? If I get sued after an accident, will the insurer provide a lawyer or help with my legal costs?
answer: Unknown from document
citation: P.23 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Third Party Fire & Theft Insurance

question: If my car gets stolen or there's an attempted theft, am I covered and what's the most you'll pay for the loss or damage?
answer: Yes. Loss or damage to your vehicle caused by theft or attempted theft is covered, and the maximum the insurer will pay for such loss or damage is $10,000.
citation: P.23 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Cover for fire & theft loss or damage

question: If my car is damaged by fire and the repairs come to more than $10,000, do you just pay the $10,000 maximum or how does that work?
answer: The insurer’s overall limit for fire damage is $10,000. They will decide whether to repair the vehicle, pay the fair and reasonable repair cost as they determine it, or pay the vehicle’s market value (less any applicable deductions), but in any case the most they will pay is $10,000.
citation: P.23 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Cover for fire & theft loss or damage

question: Can I choose to have my own mechanic repair the car, or do you decide whether to repair it, pay the repair cost, or pay the market value (and will you take my preference into account)?
answer: The insurer decides whether to repair the vehicle, pay the fair and reasonable repair cost as they determine it, or pay the market value (less any deductions). They will take the circumstances of the claim into account and will consider any preference you may have when making that decision.
citation: P.23 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Cover for fire & theft loss or damage

question: If another driver hits my car and it’s clearly their fault but they don’t have insurance, am I covered and how much will the policy pay for the damage?
answer: You may be covered by a limited benefit of up to $5,000 for damage caused by another driver who is uninsured, provided the collision was with another vehicle, the insurer is satisfied the other driver was at fault, you can supply the other vehicle’s registration and the other driver’s name and residential address, and the damage exceeds your policy’s basic excess. The insurer will decide whether to repair the vehicle, pay the fair and reasonable cost of repairs as they determine, or pay the market value of the vehicle less any applicable deductions, taking the circumstances and your preference into account.
citation: P.23 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Limited cover for accident damage to your vehicle

question: What information do I need to get from the other driver at the scene to qualify for this limited cover?
answer: You need to obtain the other vehicle’s registration details and the other driver’s name and residential address. In addition, for the limited cover to apply the insurer must be satisfied the collision was the other driver’s fault and that the driver was uninsured for your damage, and the damage must exceed your policy’s basic excess.
citation: P.23 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Limited cover for accident damage to your vehicle

question: If the other driver is insured and it’s their fault, do I have to pay any admin fees, and will you always repair my car or could you pay me cash instead?
answer: If the at-fault driver is insured and entitled to cover for your damage you must pay an administration charge of $300. The insurer will then decide whether to repair your vehicle, pay the fair and reasonable cost of repairing your vehicle as determined by them, or pay you the market value of your vehicle less any applicable deductions; they will consider the circumstances of your claim and your preference but the ultimate choice is theirs.
citation: P.23 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Limited cover for accident damage to your vehicle

question: Can I choose my own repairer or do I have to use an RACV partner — and if I pick my own, what do they need to have (licenses, equipment, equipment, etc.)?
answer: You have automatic access to RACV’s partner repairer network, but you may choose your own repairer. If you pick your own, that repairer must hold all equipment, skills, capabilities, licences and authorisations required by law to complete the repairs to your vehicle.
citation: P.24 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Any RACV Partner Repairer

question: If my car can’t be driven after an accident, will you cover towing — how many tows will you pay for and where will you tow it to?
answer: If we agree to pay your claim and your vehicle cannot be driven or is unsafe, we will pay the reasonable cost to tow it from the accident location to whichever place we decide is closer — either a repairer or other place we recommend, or a safe place. We only pay for one tow; if the vehicle needs towing more than once, we will decide which tow to cover.
citation: P.24 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Any RACV Partner Repairer

question: If my car’s stolen and isn’t driveable, will you organise a hire car for me? What’s the daily limit and maximum time you’ll cover, do you pay the hire excess/security bond, and can I arrange my own hire car or do I need your written consent?
answer: If we agree to pay a claim for theft or attempted theft and the vehicle is not driveable or is unsafe, we will arrange a hire car for you and try to meet your needs. We will pay up to $60 per day for hire, for a maximum of 21 days (or until the vehicle is recovered and repaired, or until we pay a total-loss claim — whichever is shortest). We will also pay up to $2,000 in total toward any hire-car excess and security bond payable under the hire agreement because of loss or damage. Running costs, optional extras, loss or damage to the hire car and liability from using the hire car are not covered. You must not arrange your own hire car unless you get our prior written consent; if you do so without that consent, we may decide not to pay for it.
citation: P.24 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Any RACV Partner Repairer

question: If I have to get emergency repairs after an incident, will you reimburse me and up to how much? Do I have to pay up front and keep the receipts?
answer: If the insurer agrees to pay your claim and the repairs are emergency repairs directly related to the incident, they will reimburse you for emergency repairs up to $800 in total. You generally must pay for those emergency repairs up front and provide the receipts, unless the insurer agrees otherwise.
citation: P.26 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Emergency repairs

question: How do you decide what counts as an emergency repair — is it just work to keep the car secure or to get me safely back on the road?
answer: The insurer determines what qualifies as an emergency repair. Emergency repairs are those carried out to keep the vehicle secure or to get you safely back on the road.
citation: P.26 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Emergency repairs

question: My car was damaged about 150 km from home and can’t be driven — do you cover transport and hotel for me and my pets, and will you also arrange to bring my car back home? How much would you pay and do I need to get your agreement before I book anything?
answer: If the vehicle is damaged more than 100 kilometres from home and the insurer has agreed to cover the loss or damage, they will pay up to $750 in total for reasonable costs including: transportation for you, your passengers and your domestic pets back to your home (or to another agreed destination); temporary accommodation for you, your passengers and pets (room rate only); and transportation of the vehicle either from the repair location back to your home or from the incident location to a repairer near your home. You must obtain the insurer’s agreement to those costs before they are incurred.
citation: P.26 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Emergency repairs

question: My car was towed to a storage yard after I lodged a claim — will you cover the storage fees while you wait to assess it, and do I need to get your approval before arranging anything?
answer: If we agree to pay your claim and the vehicle is taken to a storage facility or holding yard, we will pay the reasonable storage costs from the time you lodge the claim until we assess the vehicle. You must get our agreement to pay those storage costs before you make any arrangements. You may be asked to move the vehicle, and we can move it if necessary.
citation: P.26 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Storage costs

question: I had to get emergency repairs done after an incident — will you reimburse me for those, how much will you pay, and do I need to pay up front and keep the receipts?
answer: If we agree to pay your claim and the repairs are emergency repairs directly related to that incident, we will reimburse you for emergency repairs up to $800 in total. Non‑emergency repairs are not covered. We determine what qualifies as an emergency repair; the work must be to keep the vehicle secure or to get you back on the road safely. You must pay for the emergency repairs up front and provide the receipts unless we agree otherwise.
citation: P.26 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Storage costs

question: We were more than 100 km from home when our car was damaged and couldn’t be driven — will you cover transport and temporary accommodation for me, my passengers and our pets, and what’s the total you’ll pay?
answer: If the vehicle is damaged in a covered incident more than 100 kilometres from home and cannot be driven, we will pay up to $750 in total for reasonable costs toward: transportation for you, your passengers and your domestic pets to your home (or another destination if we agree), temporary accommodation (room rate only) for you, your passengers and pets, and transportation of your vehicle from where it is repaired to your home or from the incident location to a repairer near your home. We must agree to those costs before they are incurred; costs for transporting any item being towed by your vehicle at the time of the incident are not covered.
citation: P.26 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Storage costs

question: If my car gets stolen and it's not safe to drive, will you arrange a hire car for me and how much will you pay per day and for how long?
answer: If we accept your theft/attempted theft claim and your vehicle cannot be driven or is unsafe, we will arrange a hire car and pay up to $60 per day. Cover lasts for up to 21 days, or until your vehicle is recovered and repaired, or until we pay your claim if the vehicle is a total loss, whichever is shortest.
citation: P.25 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Hire car after theft or attempted theft

question: Do you cover petrol, tolls or optional extras like a GPS on the hire car?
answer: No. Running costs (for example petrol or tolls) and additional hire car costs (for example optional extras such as a GPS) are not covered.
citation: P.25 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Hire car after theft or attempted theft

question: If the hire car gets damaged, will you cover the excess or security bond, and am I personally liable for damage or third‑party claims?
answer: We will pay up to $2,000 in total for any excess and security bond payable under the hire car agreement because of loss or damage to the hire car or because it causes loss or damage. However, loss or damage to the hire car itself is not covered, and liability arising from use of the hire car is not covered, so those costs/claims are not covered by us.
citation: P.25 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Hire car after theft or attempted theft

question: If my car's in an accident and can't be driven, will you tow it to a repairer or safe place and pay the tow bill — is that paid on top of the market value you might pay for the car?
answer: If we accept your claim after an incident and your vehicle can't be driven or is unsafe, we will pay the reasonable cost to tow it to a repairer we choose or to a safe place. This towing payment is additional to any market value settlement.
citation: P.25 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Towing

question: What if my car needs to be towed more than once after the incident — do you cover multiple tows or just one tow?
answer: We only pay for one tow. If the vehicle needs to be towed more than once, we will decide which single tow to cover.
citation: P.25 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Towing

question: If my car is stolen and can't be driven, will you arrange a hire car for me, how much will you pay per day and for how long, and are running costs or damage to the hire car covered?
answer: If we accept a theft or attempted theft claim and the vehicle is undriveable or unsafe, we will arrange a hire car and try to meet your needs. We will pay up to $60 per day for the hire, for up to 21 days or until the vehicle is recovered and repaired or until we pay a total‑loss claim, whichever is shortest. We will also pay up to $2,000 in total for any hire‑car excess or security bond arising from loss or damage. We do not cover running costs (for example petrol or tolls), optional extras (for example GPS), loss or damage to the hire car itself, or liability from using the hire car.
citation: P.25 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - Towing

question: If my car gets stolen and can't be driven, will you arrange a hire car for me and what are the daily and total limits plus what hire costs aren't covered?
answer: If we accept your theft or attempted theft claim and the vehicle cannot be driven or is unsafe, we will arrange a hire car and pay up to $60 per day. Cover lasts for up to 21 days (or until the vehicle is recovered and repaired, or we pay your claim if it is a total loss), whichever is shortest. We will also pay up to $2,000 in total for any hire-car excess or security bond arising from loss or damage to the hire car. You must not arrange your own hire without our prior written consent. We do not cover running costs (for example petrol or tolls), optional extras (for example GPS), loss or damage to the hire car, or any liability from using the hire car.
citation: P.25 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - – Additional cover

question: If my vehicle needs towing after an incident, will you pay for the tow and how many tows will you cover?
answer: If we accept your claim and the vehicle cannot be driven or is unsafe, we will pay the reasonable cost to tow the vehicle from the incident location to a repairer or other place we recommend, or to a safe place. We only pay for one tow; if more than one tow is needed, we will decide which tow to pay for.
citation: P.25 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - – Additional cover

question: I had to pay for emergency repairs after an incident—will you reimburse me, how much, and what do I need to provide to get paid?
answer: If we accept your claim and you made emergency repairs because of the incident, we will reimburse you up to $800 in total for those emergency repairs. Repairs must be emergency in nature as we determine (needed to keep the vehicle secure or to get you back on the road safely), must be directly related to the incident, and you must pay for them up front and provide receipts unless we agree otherwise. Non-emergency repairs are not covered.
citation: P.25 - RACV motor-insurance-pds-current.pdf - – Additional cover
